political.s: Historically the founders supported right-wing Jewish causes and Israeli settlements, while current operations under General Mills/Nestlé show neutral corporate stance. No significant direct corporate political donations or lobbying documented under current ownership.

charity.s: Häagen-Dazs has committed to supporting underrepresented creators and communities through its #ThatsDazs campaign with $1.5M+ in donations since 2021. Focus areas include arts, music, fashion, culinary fields and women empowerment via The Rose Project.
charity.focus: Arts, music, fashion, culinary, women empowerment, underrepresented creators

environment.s: Parent company General Mills has set emissions reduction targets and sustainability goals. Häagen-Dazs has made plastic reduction commitments and sources sustainable ingredients including Rainforest Alliance vanilla and cocoa. However, lacks specific independent carbon emissions data.

labor.s: Limited specific OSHA/NLRB data found. Glassdoor ratings show 3/5 stars for compensation. Entry-level roles pay around $17-20 per hour, while management positions reach $199K+. Parent company General Mills maintains codes of conduct for suppliers.

dei.s: Häagen-Dazs has actively pursued diversity initiatives through the #ThatsDazs campaign supporting BIPOC and LGBTQ+ creators since 2021, with $1.5M+ donations and The Rose Project honoring female co-founder. Despite anti-DEI headwinds, brand maintains commitment to underrepresented communities.

animals.s: Company statement indicates animal testing occurs 'when required by law' for General Mills products. Not certified cruelty-free. Does use animal-derived ingredients (dairy, eggs, honey) in most products.

guns.s: Häagen-Dazs is an ice cream company with no manufacturing or sales of firearms. Not applicable to business model.

privacy.s: Limited publicly available privacy policy information. Parent company Nestlé and General Mills handle customer data. No major privacy breaches or complaints documented.

execPay.s: Limited executive compensation data available. Parent company General Mills reports executive compensation via SEC filings. CEO/CFO level executives likely compensated significantly higher than entry-level employees earning $17-20/hour.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
